module-6-name-badge-package.label: Module Project: Build a Name Badge Package
module-6-name-badge-package.summary: A campus club needs quick badges for a welcome table, so you will build a small multi-file package that cleans names and formats roles.

sketch0.title: A club needs badges before the event starts

mp_3.title: Document and clean the badge helper

mp-1-create-clean-name-module.title: Create the name cleaner module
mp-1-create-clean-name-module.prompt: The student welcome club has a spreadsheet full of names with messy spaces and capitalization. Create `tools/names.py` with `clean_name(text)`, import it in `main.py`, read one name from input, and print the cleaned name for the badge table.
mp-1-create-clean-name-module.hint: Put the cleaning logic in `tools/names.py`, then let `main.py` import and use it.

help.concept: A helper module keeps reusable logic out of `main.py`, which makes the badge app easier to grow.
help.hint_2: In `main.py`, import `clean_name`, read input, and print the cleaned name.

mp-1-create-clean-name-module.starterCode:
raw_name = input()

# Create tools/names.py with clean_name(text).
# Import clean_name and print the cleaned name.

mp-2-add-badge-helper.title: Add the badge helper module
mp-2-add-badge-helper.prompt: The club now wants each badge to include a role like volunteer, mentor, or guest. Keep `tools/names.py`, create `tools/badges.py` with `make_badge(name, role)`, import it in `main.py`, read a name and role, clean the name, and print the finished badge.
mp-2-add-badge-helper.hint: The badge helper should receive already-cleaned text and return one formatted string.

help.concept: This step adds a second helper file so each module has one clear job: names clean names, badges format badge text.

mp-2-add-badge-helper.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
# TODO: import make_badge from tools.badges

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
# TODO: print the badge

mp-3-document-badge-helper.title: Document and clean the badge helper
mp-3-document-badge-helper.prompt: Another volunteer will maintain this badge app next week. Keep the program working, but add clear docstrings to the helper functions so someone else can understand what each function expects and returns.
mp-3-document-badge-helper.hint: Do not change the behavior; only make the helper functions easier to understand.

help.concept: Docstrings turn working code into maintainable code by explaining the purpose, inputs, and return value of each helper.
help.hint_1: Add a triple-quoted docstring under each function definition.
help.hint_2: Keep the same imports, function names, return values, and printed output.

mp-3-document-badge-helper.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

mp-4-package-welcome-message.title: Print the final welcome badge
mp-4-package-welcome-message.prompt: The event table is ready. Start from the completed badge helpers, build the badge in `main.py`, and print one friendly final line beginning with `Welcome!` so the output feels polished for the club.
mp-4-package-welcome-message.hint: Store the badge in a variable, then use it inside a final welcome message.

help.concept: The final app uses small helper modules to make the main program read like a simple story: get input, clean it, build the badge, print the welcome message.
help.hint_1: Use `badge = make_badge(name, role)` after cleaning the name.
help.hint_2: Print a line that starts with `Welcome!` and includes the badge text.

mp-4-package-welcome-message.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
badge = make_badge(name, role)
# TODO: print a final welcome line using the badge.

try-module-6-name-badge-package-sketch0.title: Create the first badge helper file
try-module-6-name-badge-package-sketch0.prompt:
Set up the first helper for the badge app.

1. Create a folder named `tools`.
2. Create `tools/names.py`.
3. In `tools/names.py`, define `clean_name(text)`.
4. In `main.py`, import `clean_name`.
5. Print the cleaned version of `"  ava  "`.
try-module-6-name-badge-package-sketch0.hint: Put `clean_name` in `tools/names.py`, then import it in `main.py`.

help.concept: The badge project starts by moving name-cleaning into its own helper file.

try_module_6_name_badge_package_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_module_6_name_badge_package_sketch0.prompt:
Set up the first helper for the badge app.

1. Create a folder named `tools`.
2. Create `tools/names.py`.
3. In `tools/names.py`, define `clean_name(text)`.
4. In `main.py`, import `clean_name`.
5. Print the cleaned version of `"  ava  "`.

sketch-1.title: A club needs badges before the event starts
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
The student welcome club is setting up a check-in table and needs clean, consistent badges before guests arrive. Names come in with extra spaces, roles need consistent formatting, and the app should be easy for another volunteer to maintain.

You will grow the badge tool one step at a time. Each step starts from the previous canonical solution, then adds a new helper, import, docstring, or final output.
